title: Splunk On-Call (VictorOps) Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Splunk On-Call (formerly VictorOps) is an incident management and on-call scheduling platform that provides intelligent alert routing, escalation policies, and real-time collaboration for incident response. Splunk On-Call integrates with monitoring and alerting tools to ensure the right team members are notified and can collaborate effectively during incidents. The T1 Agentics integration enables SOC teams to trigger incidents, manage on-call rotations, and coordinate security incident response through Splunk On-Call's alerting infrastructure.

**Vendor:** Splunk (Cisco)
**Category:** Ticketing & Incident Management
**Authentication:** API Key

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Splunk On-Call integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- A Splunk On-Call account (Essentials, Full-Stack, or Enterprise plan)
- API access enabled for your organization
- An API key:
  - Navigate to **Integrations > API** in the Splunk On-Call portal
  - Copy the **API ID** and **API Key**
- For the REST Endpoint integration:
  - Navigate to **Integrations > REST Generic**
  - Copy the **REST Endpoint URL** (includes the routing key)
- At least one escalation policy and team configured
- Your organization slug/ID from the Splunk On-Call URL
- Network access from T1 Agentics to the Splunk On-Call API (`https://api.victorops.com`)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Splunk On-Call** or browse the **Ticketing** category
3. Click on the Splunk On-Call card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Splunk On-Call SOC")
   - **API ID**: Your Splunk On-Call API ID
   - **API Key**: Your Splunk On-Call API key
   - **REST Endpoint URL**: (Optional) For event-based incident creation
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify API access
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Create Incident | Generic | Create a new incident via the REST endpoint |
| Get Incident | Investigate | Retrieve details of a specific incident |
| List Incidents | Investigate | List incidents with phase and date filtering |
| Acknowledge Incident | Generic | Acknowledge a triggered incident |
| Resolve Incident | Generic | Resolve an active incident |
| Reroute Incident | Generic | Reroute an incident to a different team or user |
| Add Incident Timeline Entry | Generic | Add a note to the incident timeline |
| List On-Call Users | Investigate | List who is currently on call |
| List Teams | Investigate | List all teams in the organization |
| List Escalation Policies | Investigate | List all escalation policies |
| List Routing Keys | Investigate | List available routing keys |
| Get User On-Call Schedule | Investigate | Get on-call schedule for a specific user |

## Common Use Cases

- **Security alert escalation**: Trigger Splunk On-Call incidents for critical security alerts, leveraging routing keys to direct them to the appropriate security team
- **On-call SOC coordination**: Check who is currently on call for the security team before routing incidents, ensuring immediate response
- **Incident timeline documentation**: Add investigation findings and containment actions to the incident timeline, creating a real-time record of the response
- **Multi-team coordination**: Reroute incidents between security, infrastructure, and application teams as the nature of the incident becomes clearer
- **Automated incident lifecycle**: Integrate acknowledgment and resolution actions into T1 Agentics playbooks to automatically manage incident states as response progresses

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| 401 Authentication error | Verify the API ID and API Key are correct. Ensure API access is enabled for your organization |
| REST endpoint not triggering incidents | Verify the REST endpoint URL includes the correct routing key. Ensure the routing key maps to an active escalation policy |
| Incident not routing to correct team | Check the routing key configuration. Routing keys must be mapped to teams and escalation policies in the Splunk On-Call settings |
| On-call returns empty | Verify teams have active on-call schedules with assigned users. Check rotation start/end dates |
| Timeline entries not appearing | Ensure the incident exists and is in an active state. Timeline entries cannot be added to resolved incidents |
| Rate limiting | Implement request throttling. Splunk On-Call has per-minute rate limits on API calls |
